name: Maid of Sker VR
short_description: Maid of Sker VR is a first-person survival horror, set in a remote hotel with a gory and macabre history from Welsh folklore. Brave the nightmares of the Quiet Ones. Do not panic… don’t even breathe!
detailed_description:
Wales Interactive brings its award-winning survival horror to life in Maid of Sker VR.

Step inside Sker Hotel, a remote estate steeped in Welsh folklore, unsettling history, and the twisted legacy of the Williams family. With only a handheld sound device to defend yourself, you must stay hidden, stay silent, and use every shadow to survive the cult that hunts by sound.

Brave the nightmares of the Quiet Ones. Do not panic… don’t even breathe! Set in 1898 and inspired by the haunting Welsh tale of Elisabeth Williams, this is a story of a family empire driven by torture, slavery, piracy and a supernatural mystery that suffocates the grounds of the hotel.

Stay Quiet to Stay Alive

  • Experience the Quiet Ones in terrifying proximity as they patrol, search and charge based on the sounds your character makes.

  • Manage breathing and movement to stay hidden. Heavy breathing, rushing footsteps and panicked noise can attract danger.

  • Use the Phonic Modulator to stun enemies without killing them. A well timed blast buys precious seconds to escape or reach safety.

Hands On VR Horror

  • Operate the Phonic Modulator by hand with manual firing and reloading.

  • Interact with the hotel using natural VR actions: open doors, use puzzle mechanisms, pick up notes, collect dolls and examine key objects.

  • A realistic 1898 setting presented at human scale, with detailed interiors and close quarter tension.


A Welsh Folklore Nightmare

  • A narrative inspired by the Welsh tale of Elisabeth Williams and the disturbing history surrounding the Sker Hotel estate.

  • Reimagined Welsh hymns Calon Lân, Suo Gân and Ar Hyd Y Nos performed by Tia Kalmaru, as featured in the original game.

  • Multiple endings shaped by your exploration and the clues you uncover.

  • Support for 15 languages including Welsh.

supported_languages:
English*, French, Italian, German, Spanish - Spain, Arabic, Hungarian, Japanese, Korean, Polish, Portuguese - Brazil, Russian, Simplified Chinese, Turkish, Welsh
*languages with full audio support

reviews.review_text:
Give it a try!

I am 3 hours in and having a great time so far. At the beginning there was the odd missing texture (and some are muddy), and the outside could have looked much better with a different colouring but other than that a solid, bug-free experience. I did not get any stutters like mentioned in a review on yt.

The artwork, lighting and the incredible sounddesign in particular create a spine chilling, very immersive atmosphere. When I first got into the house I was just ♥♥♥♥♥ footing around not daring to touch anything in fear of triggering something bad. You can only save in certain rooms, however, they seem to be placed fair enough. Cutscenes are in 2D only, unfortunately.

With regards to gameplay I am not sure if I have fully understood the mechanics yet and had to set the game to 'easy' at some point since I kept getting caught (this being the second easiest out of 4 difficulties). I hope, they will not overdo it with the enemies as I move on.

Since the PSVR2 and the Quest versions seem to be subpar, sadly this game is getting a lot of bad reviews today. Don't let this keep you from trying the pc version. It is a good vr game imo, and I am excited to see what happens next.

Another positive: It worked out of the box with my HP Reverb G1 (Oasis driver).

The game works perfectly with Index controllers - hands are properly aligned and all buttons work as intended.

Seems devs made some great work - the game looks great with some dynamic shadows, many high-res textures, great lighting, fog effects, nice reflections, nice water effects and much more. The PCVR version is 15GB, the Quest version is just 3GB - surely PCVR is the premium version. PSVR2 owners are troubled by lack of foveated rendering and blurry image quality - but the PCVR version looks razor-sharp using TAA and high level of super-sampling.

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3686964776
I'm getting some vibes of The Vanishing of Ethan Carter with this adventure game - but seems some vibes from Resident Evil and the like will be added later - judging from the launch trailer. So far I've met a few enemies, but easy to cover your mouth to let them not hear you. The gameplay is rather limited, but interesting. 

This does seem like a work of passion - congrats to the devs for making a seemingly awesome VR game (I'm only 1 hour in, but having a great time). Being a 2D port, only few things can be interacted with - but you can open doors, pick-up and manipulate important objects, much like in The Midnight Walk. There is snap and smooth turning, head or controller oriented movement, I do not have any big issues with the controls. Having played nearly 2 hours, my rating would be 6.5 - 7/10.

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3686956377
The game uses TAA, but image quality is really sharp forcing Index res 300% - and I need some motion smoothing for that with an RTX 3090, but this is an adventure (horror) game, motion smoothing works great. 

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3686956710
(No issues on PC) - PCVR Impressions on RTX5080: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ihLGqjfXq2Q
